TextENGNew: Shion and I decided to appeal to the adults closest to us about Satoko's situation, so we headed for the staff room.
``Oh, what's the matter, Maebara-kun?@k@vS18/22/161400002. And the class representative is with you too -- is something troubling you?``
Before Chie-sensei could say anything more, Shion leaned forward.
``I'm sorry, but I'm not actually Mion.@k@vS18/06/160600081. I'm her younger sister, Shion.``
``O-oh, is that so...?``
Chie-sensei was clearly flustered.
Not that I could blame her. Even I had doubted Shion's existence more than once in the past.
``You look so alike, I'm afraid I was mistaken...``
But without addressing Chie-sensei's confusion, Shion pressed on without a moment's hesitation.
``There's something you need to hear.@k@vS18/06/160600083. It's an urgent matter.``
The desperate intensity in Shion's eyes made Chie-sensei instinctively straighten her posture.
``Satoko Houjou is in a deeply troubled living situation with her uncle, Teppei Houjou.``
``That's quite a serious claim...@k@vS18/22/161400006. Do you have any evidence to support this?``
``Sensei, you're certainly aware that Satoko has been absent from school for quite some time, aren't you?``
``That's because... as I've informed the class, she's been out sick.@k@vS18/22/161400008. Her guardian has properly reported it to us.``
``That man is her guardian?@k@vS18/06/160600087. Are you seriously saying that?!``
Undisturbed by Shion's confrontational attitude, Chie-sensei maintained her composure.
``I'm aware that rumors and speculation have been circulating.@k@vS18/22/161400010. That's precisely why we need to respond calmly at this stage.``
``So you're just going to leave Satoko to her fate and do nothing?``
``No. First, I intend to visit the Houjou household and speak with them directly about the situation.``
``He'll just put on the act of a proper guardian and talk his way out of it!``
``Shion, calm down.@k@vS18/01/160100076. Let's hear what Sensei has to say.``
We were letting our childish sense of justice run away with us.
Emotions alone weren't going to save Satoko. What we needed right now was practical support.
Besides, Chie-sensei might... she just might be willing to act on this. I wanted to place my last hope on that possibility.
``You're not the kind of person who would dismiss this out of hand, Sensei.@k@vS18/01/160100078. I know you'll give Satoko's situation the serious attention it deserves.``
``That's right.@k@vS18/22/161400013. It's not like I have a boyfriend to distract me right now, so the least I can do is look after my precious students.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Shion let out a faint smile and relaxed her shoulders.
``I understand...``
``If I observe even the slightest sign of a problem, I intend to take decisive action.``
Shion frowned with open skepticism. Trying to reassure her, Chie-sensei placed a hand on her shoulder and continued.
``Sometimes, it's important to trust others and rely on them.@k@vS18/22/161400023. I believe that takes courage too.``
``We can trust you on this, right, Sensei?``
``Have I ever lied to you?``
I slowly shook my head. We hadn't known each other long enough to call it a deep relationship, but she had never lied. ...At least, I was fairly certain of that.
``Please, leave this matter to me.@k@vS18/22/161400026. Maebara-kun and... the class representative's sister should try not to worry.``
Chie-sensei thumped her chest as if to say 'count on me.'
Though I had ostensibly accepted Chie-sensei's words--
After witnessing firsthand the cold, reflexive reaction of the 'adults,' we couldn't hide our disappointment.
We couldn't entrust everything to 'adults' who so deftly separated their true feelings from their public facade.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: We had to rescue Satoko with our own hands...
Unable to stay still any longer, Shion and I set off for the Houjou house where Satoko was.
A searing sense of urgency consumed my mind, leaving no room for anything else.
I pressed forward like a mechanical doll, mindlessly repeating the motion of putting one foot in front of the other.
What was I even planning to do once I saw Satoko...?
Was I really going to charge in with no plan, no strategy, grab Satoko by the hand and run?
Playing hero... In a way, maybe Mion had been right about me.
Even if I recklessly charged in waving the banner of justice, a neat and tidy 'happily ever after' wasn't waiting for us.
...Enough already! Stop overthinking this!
I'd do what I could. That alone should be enough.
``Kei-chan!``
The sudden call of my name snapped me back to reality.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: When I turned around, Shion was standing far behind me in the distance.
``What are you doing back there, Shion?``
``Um... I'm sorry to rain on your parade, but...``
``What is it?@k@vS18/01/160100083. We don't have time to dawdle around here!``
``Um... just to check...@k@vS18/06/160600098. you do know where Satoko's house is, right?``
Huh?
``Satoko's house is this way.``
Shion pointed in the opposite direction from where I'd been heading.
``...I-is that so?@k@vS18/01/160100085. Sorry... I just had this image of it being past the shrine, and...``
I was stumbling over my words.
Where exactly had I been planning to go, walking all that way without even knowing where the destination was?
I'd been so frantic that I'd completely lost sight of my surroundings.
``Heh heh heh heh...``
Seeing my face turn beet red, Shion couldn't hold back her laughter.
``No, b-besides!@k@vS18/01/160100087. I was thinking of stopping by my house first, and...``
My masculine pride was at stake, so against my better judgment, I kept piling on the excuses.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: What did my masculine pride even count for at a time like this? I wondered, even as I kept talking...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``F-for now, let's have some tea and catch our breath...@k@vS18/01/160100089. Barging in looking for a fight won't solve anything.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Hee hee hee hee hee...``
``W-what?``
``But Kei-chan, your house isn't in that direction either, is it?``
I'd dug myself in too deep with the excuses.
What was I even thinking...? All that fired-up energy was just spinning its wheels.
``I'm sorry for laughing.@k@vS18/06/160600106. But now I know that you're someone who burns really hot, Kei-chan.``
``Mion... what about her?``
``It's nothing.@k@vS18/06/160600109. Come on, let's hurry to Satoko's house.@k@vS18/06/160600110. We're wasting time.``
Putting on a smile as if to smooth things over, Shion beckoned me forward.
``Shall I hold your hand so you don't get lost?``
``Give me a break already...``
``Hee hee hee hee hee... You're such a shy one, Kei-chan.``
``Sorry for being such a kid.``
``No, honestly. Your sincerity and straightforwardness -- I truly envy those qualities, Kei-chan.``
``Somehow that sounds like you're killing me with kindness.``
``I mean it sincerely.@k@vS18/06/160600115. ...But please, be careful.``
``Be careful...?``
``...Someone could be following us...@k@vS18/06/160600117. It's not impossible, you know...``
Her warning, delivered in a low tone, suddenly filled me with unease.
That couldn't be...
But...
I had the distinct feeling that someone was watching me from the shadows.
A faint smirk seeming to float in the darkness...
Was it just my imagination...? Or...?
The Houjou house sat alone at the edge of a dense thicket, past the peaceful countryside scenery.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Thinking of Satoko enduring her suffering alone inside those walls filled me with a fierce, churning anger.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I rang the doorbell, but there was no answer.
An eerie silence enveloped the Houjou house.
Could it be...
A dark premonition drove me forward.
``Hold on... if you keep mashing it like that, you'll break it.``
Shion's warning didn't even register.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: I pounded the bell again and again, pouring out my anger and anxiety.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But still, no response came.
I hadn't come all this way to turn back empty-handed!
I decided to force my way in.
``Kei-chan.@k@vS18/06/160600120. If you go any further, it'll be trespassing.``
``Hah! Police, whatever -- I don't care anymore!!``
``...You're right. Bring it on!``
The kind of battle cry you'd expect stitched onto a biker gang's jacket -- coming from Shion's mouth, of all people...
I felt like I'd just witnessed something I wasn't supposed to see.
But Shion, already rolling up her sleeves, looked incredibly dependable.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Shion and I held our breath and circled around to the garden.
A window seemed to be open -- the living room curtains were swaying.
I ducked behind the hedge and peered intently past the fluttering curtains.
A gentle breeze blew through, and the curtain billowed upward.
Beyond it... there was Satoko!
Satoko......!
I couldn't hold back any longer!
I sprang to my feet.
``Please, wait.``
Shion planted herself firmly in my path.
You're going to stop me now, of all times, Shion?
Just as I opened my mouth to argue...
``...At a time like this, isn't it supposed to be 'ladies first'?!``
Her unexpected words left me momentarily speechless.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Shion, meanwhile, flashed me a sly grin. ...I see -- she was telling me to keep my cool.
She was right. Losing my composure now would mean losing everything.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I took a deep breath to steady my nerves.
Then, once I'd calmed myself, I vaulted over the hedge, approached the house, and -- with a surge of resolve -- stepped inside!
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``Huuuh? Who the hell are you punks?!@k@vS18/21/162000018. Barging in like you own the place -- what the hell do you think yer doing?!``
Facing down Teppei's vulgar snarl, I threw the curtains aside.
His threats held no power over me anymore.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I just wanted to save Satoko. That single thought alone was holding me up.
``......Keiichi-san!``
``Satoko...``
But just then, the sliding door to the next room opened without warning.
The stale smell of cigarette smoke drifted out.
The man standing in the doorway spoke without even glancing our way.
``Tecchan.@k@vS18/00/yakuA16002. Grab me another beer, will ya?``
Inside, several men in an obvious state of drunkenness sat around a mahjong table, bottles in hand.
These bastards... these bastards... Satoko was suffering right there on the other side of a single sliding door, and they couldn't have cared less...!
These people weren't human... they were rotten to the core...!
In that instant, fury consumed me.
I re-clenched my sweat-slicked fists and stepped forward.
The men filed out of the living room to back up Teppei.
They surrounded Shion and me.
``What d'ya kids think yer doing in someone else's house?@k@vS18/00/yakuB16002. This here's Tecchan's place, y'know.``
``Punks these days, huh?@k@vS18/00/yakuC16002. Ooh, I'm sooo scared.``
``World's going to hell.``
changeReason: New translation condenses the original, omitting contextual details.

TextENGNew: ``You've got some nerve!!@k@vS18/01/160100108. It's adults like you, running around unchecked, that are rotting this world from the inside out!``
``Whaddya say?@k@vS18/00/yakuB16004. Keep runnin' that mouth around us grown-ups, and we ain't gonna stay quiet.@k@vS18/00/yakuB16005. Got that, punk?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``You bastards!@k@vS18/01/160100111. You won't get away with this!!``
I was past the point of patience or restraint. The fire that had ignited in my heart spread through my entire body, and I lunged at Teppei with lightning speed.
But I was hopelessly outnumbered.
All four of them piled on me at once.
At that moment, I cursed nothing more bitterly than my body -- a 'child's' body, inferior to an adult's in both size and strength.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Guhh!!
``Acting all high and mighty...!``
``Lemme teach ya what happens when ya mess with me... I'll make sure ya never forget...!``
Repulsive laughter split my ears.
I forced my eyes open a crack...
Teppei was making a show of winding up his fist.
Don't you dare lose here! Show them what you're made of, Keiichi Maebara!!
I arched my body back and shoved Teppei with every ounce of strength I had.
The force sent Teppei crashing into the china cabinet.
He must have hit it hard, because he writhed in pain, but his eyes blazed with fury as he staggered back to his feet.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``You little shit...!!@k@vS18/00/yakuA16007. I'll kill youuuu!!!``
I somehow managed to stand, but someone grabbed my arm and yanked me back.
They pinned me to the floor and the beating didn't stop.
The pain was too much. I turned my gaze away.
In the corner of the room, Shion had her arms around Satoko's shoulders, rubbing her back and whispering, 'It's okay. It's okay.'
Satoko was staring into the distance with vacant eyes. Her mouth hung slightly open...
Someone was pounding furiously on the front door.
Satoko's eyes snapped wide open as she turned toward the door.
Whoever it was seemed to be trying to wrench the locked handle open.
``It's Irie.@k@vS18/10/161000002. Is everything all right?@k@vS18/10/161000003. Is anyone in there?``
...The Coach's voice...? It seemed the visitor at the door was Irie.
Shion immediately leapt to her feet and dashed for the front door.
``Hold iiiiit!@k@vS18/21/162000035. If you open that door, I'll kill youuuuu!!``
Teppei's empty threats echoed through the room in vain.
But Shion was faster -- she unlocked the door and threw it open, pulling the visitor inside.
Led by Shion, Irie appeared.
Irie stood stunned at the scene before him.
``Wh-what is all this...?``
``...Oh my, if it isn't Dr. Irie.@k@vS18/21/162000037. Can I help you with somethin'?@k@vS18/21/162000038. You really didn't have to come all this way.``
``I happened to be driving past... when I heard terrible sounds coming from your house...@k@vS18/10/161000006. What on earth... is going on here?``
``Yer shoes are dirtyin' up the place.@k@vS18/21/162000040. Heeey, Satoko!@k@vS18/21/162000041. Stop standing there like a lump and get the doctor some slippers!``
``Something stylish to match the good doctor's taste.@k@vS18/21/162000043. We don't have nothin' fancy enough, but hey, it's the thought that counts, right?``
``That won't be necessary.@k@vS18/10/161000008. More importantly, I'd like to hear an explanation of this situation.``
``...It's nothing, really. These kids just barged in on their own and started causing trouble.@k@vS18/21/162000045. Kids these days, am I right?``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``I find it hard to believe that these children would ransack a room like this...``
``Well, y'see, I lost my temper a bit.@k@vS18/21/162000047. I'm old-fashioned, y'know? Sometimes my hand slips...``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: Faced with Irie's unwavering composure, Teppei began to falter.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I breathed a sigh of relief.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Now that Irie was here, surely he would save Satoko.
Irie was different from the other 'adults.'
He was someone who genuinely cared about Satoko.
The very next instant, words I never expected froze me in my tracks.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``It's nothing.``
It was Satoko. She fixed me with focused, clear eyes as I stood there in disbelief.
As if commanding me not to say another word...
``Satoko... why are you saying something like that...?``
``Keiichi-san and Shion-san are simply under a misunderstanding.@k@vS18/04/160400009. There is absolutely nothing to worry about.@k@vS18/04/160400011. Please, go home.``
``Satoko!@k@vS18/01/160100114. This is no time to be putting on a brave face!``
``Did you not hear me?@k@vS18/04/160400012. I said please go home!``
Satoko's eyes blazed with even fiercer intensity as they bore into mine.
But what they conveyed was the very opposite of her words.
Thank you, Keiichi-san. But you've done enough already...
The corner of Satoko's mouth lifted ever so slightly, as if to form a smile.
Is there truly nothing... nothing at all I can do for you...?
All you have to do is nod, and I'd charge headlong into fire itself.
So please... don't tell me to give up on saving you...
*Scrape scrape scrape scrape...*
A faint sound reached my ears. A disturbing sound.
I whipped around -- and there stood Shion.
*Scrape scrape scrape scrape... crunch!*
Shion was grinding her teeth.
With enough force that they might crack...
Like forcing a dull knife through a thick slab of meat...
``Maebara-san, Shion-san.@k@vS18/10/161000011. Let's take our leave for today. All right?``
At Irie's urging, we reluctantly left the Houjou house.
As if to mock our retreat, Teppei's crude laughter echoed behind us.
In that moment, for the very first time in my life, I knew what it meant to truly hate someone.
I hated him so intensely that my thoughts alone could have cursed him to death.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Hatred, frustration, despair... and an unspeakable sorrow tore through my heart.
Shion and I ended up getting a ride home in Irie's car.
Soothing classical music played from the radio.
But I couldn't hear a note of it.
Satoko's voice saying 'It's nothing' played over and over in my ears, fading and returning, fading and returning.
Shion sat in silence as well.
She gazed out the window at the passing scenery, but her eyes seemed to be looking at something far away, somewhere in the distant past.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ...What would become of Satoko from here on?@k And those of us who were powerless to help -- how would we change in the days ahead?
I knew Satoko needed a helping hand.@k ...But forcing a rescue would only be a temporary fix.
It was infuriating and hard to accept, but as outsiders, there was very little room for us to intervene in a family matter.

Worse yet, according to Rena, Satoko had fabricated denials before, which meant the Okinomiya consultation center -- the very people who should have had the authority to help -- were now hesitant to act.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: It's hard to trust someone who has lied to you once... I understood that well enough.@k ...But at this rate, there was no hope of any resolution, or even progress.
...A wretched feeling gnawed away at my heart like an accusation. The more I thought about it... the darker the ending I could foresee.
...No one had spoken for a long while.@k Perhaps because we had all come to the painful realization that... putting anything into words wouldn't change a thing.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``It's pointless to even try...@k@vS18/02/160200017. That's what you think, isn't it?@k@vS18/02/160200018. Mii-chan.``
``...I wouldn't go that far, but the situation isn't exactly easy...``
The exchange between Rena and Mion from earlier surfaced in my mind.@k Rena, lashing out in exasperation, and Mion, voicing resigned passivity.
At the time, I'd been disgusted by both of them and pushed back in anger -- then charged off to the Houjou house with Shion.@k ...But in the end, what had we accomplished?@k What had we changed?
Was what we did... actually nothing more than useless, one-sided self-satisfaction -- something that didn't help Satoko at all...?
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...As if sensing my unspoken question, Irie offered a denial, though his expression was complicated.@k ...But the vague, carefully chosen words he strung together only made it clearer that his true feelings lay elsewhere.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Then... seeing us sit in wordless dejection, he must have realized partway through that such diplomatic platitudes were meaningless.@k After letting out a single sigh, Irie turned to face us directly.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``...It's sad... unbearably cruel, but...@k@vS18/10/161000020_2. ......Our determination, our efforts, our devotion... they all have... limits.``

``I understand your frustration, Maebara-san. I know you can't accept this.@k@vS18/10/161000021. And I care about Satoko-chan too -- deeply.@k@vS18/10/161000022. But there are times when the most important thing we can do is simply pray.``
``If you truly want Satoko to be happy, why won't you take her away from that man?@k@vS18/01/160100120. What you're saying makes no sense, Coach!``
Unable to contain my surging emotions, I threw those unreasonable words at him, fully aware of their unfairness.@k But Irie maintained his calm and continued in a quiet tone.
``But... what actually constitutes happiness for her...?@k@vS18/10/161000024. For outsiders like us to presume to decide that -- wouldn't that be the height of arrogance?``
``We can't just sit around praying and hoping for the best... *cough*... ugh... *cough cough*!!``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: As I broke into a coughing fit from the agitation, Irie offered me a pristine white handkerchief.@k ...I gripped it tight, and mingled with my coughs came a crushing wave of frustration and sorrow that clawed at my throat.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``It's all right. It's going to be all right...``
...Even that small kindness felt grating to me right now, and only deepened my sense of wretchedness.
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``I can't do anything for Satoko...@k@vS18/01/160100123. Uugh... and that's what kills me...``
Against my will, tears began to fall.
``It's all right. It's going to be all right...@k@vS18/10/161000028. When faced with pain that defies all reason, the only solace a human being can find... is in letting their emotions run free.``
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: ``What am I even saying...@k@vS18/10/161000032. I've already forfeited any right to say such things...``
A self-deprecating smile crossed Irie's face.
Behind his glasses, I thought I caught a sharp glint in his eyes.
``What are you talking about...?``
``...I'll tell you the truth, Maebara-san.``
``The reason Satoko-chan endures that environment without trying to escape it...@k@vS18/10/161000035. ...It's atonement.``
Atonement?!
``Satoko-chan believes she's the one responsible for Satoshi-kun's disappearance.@k@vS18/10/161000037. So she sees her life with her uncle as punishment she must endure.``
What... was he saying...?
Satoshi disappearing was that bastard's doing, wasn't it?
Why should Satoko have to bear the weight of a sin she never committed?
``...That doesn't make anyone happy!!``
Shion's scream tore through the car.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ``...Not a single person is happy with this...@k@vS18/06/160600132. If only Satoshi-kun were here... if only Satoshi-kun...!``
Large teardrops streamed down Shion's cheeks as she sobbed in anguish.
I couldn't think of a single thing to say to her...
For all my big talk, in the end I hadn't been able to do a thing.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: How was I any different from the adults who chose to stand by in silence, or from Mion who had dismissed it all as futile with a single word?
Feelings alone weren't enough... They meant nothing unless I could translate them into action.
There was one -- and only one -- method that could save Satoko by force, without fail.
But that method was far too dangerous.
It was something I couldn't attempt with anything less than total commitment.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: But the die had already been cast.
I couldn't leave Satoko's fate to someone else's roll of the dice.
Then I had no choice but to roll them myself.
The 'ichi' in Keiichi means 'one' -- and it's do or die, all or nothing!
changeReason: New translation expands on the original with additional detail that alters reader understanding.

TextENGNew: I rolled an invisible die in my palm, then squeezed my fist shut around it.
An all-or-nothing gamble.
Even if luck was on my side... or even if it wasn't... someone might end up dead...
changeReason: Translation uses different phrasing that shifts the reader's understanding of the scene.

TextENGNew: The thought made me shudder involuntarily. Exposed to a nameless terror, my heart pounded faster and faster...
